title: Fixing Middleware Order for a Secure Express API with JWT Authentication

statement.markdown: In this task, you are given an Express API that uses JWT (JSON Web Token) authentication to protect its routes. The API has a middleware chain that checks for the presence of a valid JWT token in the request headers. However, the middleware chain has been misordered, causing the token to be validated before the request body is parsed. This has resulted in a subtle bug where the API returns incorrect responses to certain requests. Your task is to fix the middleware order to ensure that the request body is parsed before the JWT token is validated.
statement.outputFormat: The API should return a JSON response with the correct data
statement.notes: Time complexity: O(n), where n is the number of middleware functions

examples.input:
GET /users HTTP/1.1
Host: example.com
Authorization: Bearer my-token
examples.explanation: The API should return a JSON response with the users data

examples.input:
POST /users HTTP/1.1
Host: example.com
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer my-token
{"name": "John Doe"}
examples.explanation: The API should return a JSON response with the success message

hints.text: Check the order of the middleware functions

editorial.title: Fixing Middleware Order for a Secure Express API with JWT Authentication
editorial.intuition: The middleware chain should be ordered such that the request body is parsed before the JWT token is validated
editorial.bruteForce: Try different orders of the middleware functions
editorial.betterSolution: Use the built-in Express middleware functions to parse the request body
editorial.optimalSolution: Use the built-in Express middleware functions to parse the request body and then the JWT token validation middleware
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The API should return the correct responses to the requests
editorial.dryRun: Try running the API with different requests to test the middleware order

testCases.input:
GET /users HTTP/1.1
Host: example.com
Authorization: Bearer my-token

POST /users HTTP/1.1
Host: example.com
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer my-token
{"name": "John Doe"}
